pages.text:
Malvern Theatre, + lunch, we brought William + Peter home.

	Saturday we spent at home. On Sunday at 3.10. we left for the Cotswolds. We have come to __Enslow__, 4 miles from Woodstock. It is next to the canal + a railway bridge. Very pretty. Rain, hail in the evening.

	__Monday May 29th__ we woke to brilliant blue sky. Hated clouds came. After breakfast we walked to __Tackley__. We walked along the canal + saw a boat using the lock. Tonic loved the walk. There was a very muddy path by a mill, with a torrential river. We came over a hill to Tackley station. At the playground we tried to fly Charles kite, unsuccessfully. We had a good lunch in the pub, [?Gardners] then returned to the station to meet Thomas off the [B]irmingham train. He was playing

in a Concert the [her] evening before. We walked back to the campsite. Sunny + warm. David + Sally + Maia + [Dylan] arrived at 6.
	We had supper. The new baby, Jem, is gorgeous. The children played foot ball.

	__Tuesday May 30th__
	We woke to sun again. Thomas left at 7 to walk to Tackley for his 8 am train to London.
	In the morning we walked along the canal the other way, all except for William, who stayed at the van to revise for his physics A level. We passed locks The walk was first along the canal, then the River Cherwell, then the canal. We saw a rat + a heron, goslings, ducklings, cootlings. We had lunch at the Boatsmen Pub at __Thrupp__.
	After returning to the van, David made tea for all of us + then we set off home, David to [h]
